cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. I need to figure out what the author's main purpose is in writing this text. The options are A, B, C, or D. First, I'll read through the text again to get a better understanding. The text is about a teen travel program offered by Global Teen. It mentions various European countries like Italy, Spain, France, Austria, Germany, and others. The program is for teens and high school students, offering tours, cultural experiences, and language camps. The author emphasizes that it's an adventure and a unique experience they shouldn't miss. They also talk about the program's varying lengths, from two weeks to the whole summer break. The text ends with a call to action: "Travel with Global Teen this summer! Have an adventure... and have the time of your life this summer!" Now, looking at the options: A) Persuade teens to join a teen travel program B) Show the fun of traveling in European countries C) Compare different cultures of European countries D) Give background info about European countries The text is clearly promoting the Global Teen travel program. The author uses persuasive language like "adventure you cannot pass up," "experience of your young life that you don't want to miss," and "have the time of your life this summer!" These are all designed to convince teens to join the program.
